sub_points: Course positioning within Pattern Recognition (EE5907) and expectations for Week 7
Module outline and logistics for the lecture sequence
Continuous assessment structure and evaluation components
Primary textbooks, reference materials, and how to use them for study

syllabus.topic: Pattern Representation Learning and Feature Extraction (Motivation & Use Cases)
sub_points: Role of representation learning in pattern recognition pipelines (from raw data to decision making)
Feature extraction goals: dimensionality reduction, noise suppression, invariances, and better separability
Application examples where feature learning is essential (e.g., images, signals, high-dimensional measurements)
Unsupervised feature learning as a setting: learning structure from unlabeled data

syllabus.topic: Principal Component Analysis (PCA): Concepts and Geometric Intuition
sub_points: Definition and purpose of PCA as an unsupervised linear dimensionality reduction method
Geometric interpretation: variance maximization, orthogonal directions, projections onto subspaces
Principal components as new axes capturing maximum data spread in descending order
Connection between projection, information retention, and low-dimensional embeddings

syllabus.topic: PCA: Algebraic Formulation, Derivation, and Computation
sub_points: Algebraic definition of principal components via optimization (maximize variance under orthonormal constraints)
Derivation linking PCA to eigen-decomposition of the covariance matrix and/or SVD of the data matrix
Step-by-step computation workflow: centering, covariance formation, eigenvectors/eigenvalues, choosing k
Practical computation considerations: numerical stability, high-dimensional settings, and SVD-based PCA
Worked examples illustrating manual/algorithmic PC computation

syllabus.topic: PCA: Visualization, Reconstruction, and Use in Classification
sub_points: Visualization of PCs and interpretation of loading vectors / directions of variation
Low-rank reconstruction using top-k PCs; reconstruction error and variance explained intuition
Using PCA as a preprocessing step for classification (feature reduction before a classifier)
Key remarks and practical tips (e.g., scaling/centering and selecting number of components)
Limitations: linearity, sensitivity to scaling/outliers, interpretability, and inability to capture parts-based structure

syllabus.topic: Nonnegative Matrix Factorization (NMF): Motivation, Formulation, and Optimization
sub_points: Motivation for NMF as an unsupervised, parts-based, interpretable representation with nonnegativity constraints
Matrix factorization model X ≈ WH and meaning of basis matrix W and coefficient matrix H
Objective functions (e.g., Frobenius norm / divergence-based losses) and constraints
Optimization approaches: gradient descent viewpoint and multiplicative update rules
Worked examples computing/estimating W and H and interpreting the resulting factors

syllabus.topic: NMF: Basis Visualization, Reconstruction, and Comparative Discussion
sub_points: Visualizing learned basis components (e.g., basis images) and interpreting parts-based factors
Reconstruction of data using W and H; comparison to PCA-style reconstruction
Discussion of when NMF is preferable (nonnegative data, interpretability) and typical trade-offs (nonconvexity, local minima)
Summary of key takeaways contrasting PCA vs. NMF for representation learning
Recommended papers for deeper understanding of PCA/NMF and representation learning
Guidance for independent study: what to focus on (theory, algorithms, applications)
Pointers for connecting these methods to later topics in pattern recognition and modern feature learning
